# Rejecting Versions
*or what to Do When Gradle Refuses a Version on Purpose*

This scenario demonstrates rejecting a specific version.

#### Dependency Graph
```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 4.4
graph LR
    Root[Project Root] --> LibA[lib-a:1.0.0]
    Root -- reject --> LibA_Bad[lib-a:2.0.0]
```

#### Gradle (Groovy DSL)

```{.groovy include="/demos/gradle-demo/scenario-14-reject/build.gradle" snippet="s14-deps"}
```

#### Expected Intuition
"Don't use 2.0.0."

#### Actual Resolution Results
*   **Gradle**: Avoids `2.0.0`.

#### Classpath Reality
`1.0.0`.

#### Why This Happens
Rejection constraint.

#### How Developers Commonly "Fix" This
N/A

#### Safer Ways to Take Control
Use `reject`.

#### Signals to Watch For
Build failure if no other version exists.

#### How This Scales in Real Systems
Good for blacklisting bad releases.

#### Key Takeaway
You can blacklist versions.

#### Related Scenarios
*   [Scenario 6: Strict](../scenario-6-forcing/guide.md)
